overview_intro: Inspect the last 7 days, all-time totals, and recent activity across the system.
overview_empty_desc: Try again. If audit logs are empty, the daily summary and recent activity will stay at 0.
overview_daily_trend_empty_desc: The chart will appear here once audit activity is recorded across the last 7 days.
overview_summary_desc: Current cumulative totals across the whole system, independent of the 7-day trend window.
overview_daily_reports_desc: Key actions for the last {{days}} days, grouped by UTC calendar day.
overview_recent_events_desc: Latest audit events in reverse chronological order.
overview_background_tasks_desc: Recent recorded background task executions, including system periodic jobs and workspace jobs.
overview_system_health_unknown: System health has not run yet
overview_system_health_unknown_desc: Wait for the next scheduled check or open the task history to inspect runtime events.
overview_system_health_no_summary: No system health summary is available yet.
overview_recent_events_empty_desc: Recent audit events will appear here once activity is recorded.
overview_background_tasks_empty_desc: Recent background task records will appear here after system or workspace jobs run.
users_intro: Manage user accounts, roles, and storage quotas.
teams_intro: Create, review, and archive team workspaces across the system.
policies_intro: Configure storage policies, upload modes, and default file placement.
remote_nodes_intro: Register follower nodes from the primary console, prepare the enrollment record first, then generate a one-time enroll command for the follower server.
policy_groups_intro: Group storage policies into ordered routing rules, and bind each user to exactly one policy group.
shares_intro: Review and manage active share links across the system.
tasks_intro: Review every recorded background task across system jobs, personal workspaces, and team workspaces.
locks_intro: Inspect active WebDAV locks and clear them when necessary.
no_filtered_tasks: No background tasks match these filters
no_filtered_tasks_desc: Adjust the filters or clear them to review the full task history.
task_cleanup_desc: Only completed task records are removed. Pending, processing, and retry tasks stay untouched.
task_cleanup_finished_before: Clean up tasks completed before this time
task_cleanup_confirm_desc: This deletes task records completed before {{finishedBefore}} with kind "{{kind}}" and status "{{status}}".
task_cleanup_confirm_desc_invalid: Choose a valid completion time before continuing.
no_tasks_desc: Recorded background task history will appear here after jobs start running.
settings_intro: Adjust built-in runtime settings by category, and keep advanced overrides in the custom config area.
settings_category_user_desc: Runtime settings for user-facing account features such as avatars, sign-up, and future third-party login options.
settings_category_auth_desc: Cookie security policy plus access-token and refresh-token lifetimes.
settings_category_mail_desc: SMTP and sender settings for registration activation, email change confirmation, and other transactional mail.
settings_subcategory_mail_config_desc: SMTP connectivity, sender identity, and test delivery for transactional email.
settings_subcategory_mail_template_desc: Subjects and HTML bodies for registration, password reset, and email change messages. View magic variables next to the corresponding HTML body field.
mail_template_variables_dialog_desc: Both the subject and HTML body of the same template can use these variables. They are replaced automatically before sending, and HTML body values are safely escaped.
mail_template_variables_dialog_empty: No magic variables are available right now.
settings_template_variable_verification_: The confirmation link for the current verification email.
settings_template_variable_reset_url_des: The password reset link for the current email.
settings_template_variable_previous_emai: The user's old email address before the change.
settings_template_variable_new_email_des: The new email address the user is switching to.
mail_send_test_email_hint: Defaults to the current admin's email address, but you can change the recipient before sending.
mail_test_email_dialog_desc: You can edit the recipient address before sending. Leave it blank to fall back to the current admin's email.
settings_category_network_desc: Cross-origin access rules, preflight caching, and related network behavior.
settings_category_operations_desc: Runtime knobs for dispatch cadence, maintenance scheduling, and administrative list limits.
settings_subcategory_operations_backgrou: Separate concurrency caps for background task lanes so slow archive work cannot block quick thumbnail jobs.
settings_category_storage_desc: Version retention, cleanup policies, and default storage quota.
settings_category_webdav_desc: Control whether the WebDAV entrypoint is exposed.
settings_category_audit_desc: Audit logging switches and retention windows.
settings_category_general_desc: Site-wide public branding and shared deployment behavior.
settings_category_other_desc: System settings that do not belong to a named category.
settings_item_auth_cookie_secure_desc: Controls whether auth and share-verification cookies are only sent over HTTPS. You can disable it for temporary plain-HTTP internal testing, but it should stay enabled in production.
settings_item_auth_allow_user_registrati: Controls whether new users can create accounts from the public sign-up flow. When disabled, only the initial setup flow and admin-created accounts remain available.
settings_item_auth_register_activation_e: Controls whether new users must confirm their email address before they can sign in for the first time.
settings_item_auth_access_token_ttl_secs: Controls how long newly issued access tokens stay valid after login or refresh.
settings_item_auth_refresh_token_ttl_sec: Controls how long refresh tokens stay valid before users must sign in again.
settings_item_auth_register_activation_t: Controls how long registration activation links remain usable before they expire.
settings_item_auth_contact_change_ttl_se: Controls how long email change confirmation links remain usable before they expire.
settings_item_auth_password_reset_ttl_se: Controls how long password reset links remain usable before they expire.
settings_item_auth_contact_verification_: The minimum wait between verification email resend attempts for the same user.
settings_item_auth_password_reset_reques: The minimum wait between password reset email requests for the same user.
settings_item_mail_smtp_host_desc: SMTP server hostname used to send transactional mail.
settings_item_mail_smtp_port_desc: SMTP server port used to send transactional mail.
settings_item_mail_smtp_username_desc: SMTP username used when authenticated delivery is enabled.
settings_item_mail_smtp_password_desc: SMTP password or app-specific token used for authenticated delivery.
settings_item_mail_from_address_desc: Sender email address used for activation and contact verification messages.
settings_item_mail_from_name_desc: Sender display name shown to recipients of transactional mail.
settings_item_mail_security_desc: Controls whether SMTP uses an encrypted connection. When enabled, port 465 uses implicit SSL/TLS and other ports default to STARTTLS. When disabled, SMTP is sent in plain text.
settings_item_mail_template_register_act: Subject template for registration activation emails.
HTML template for registration activation emails. A complete HTML document is recommended for best client compatibility.
settings_item_mail_template_contact_chan: Subject template for email change confirmation emails.
HTML template for email change confirmation emails. A complete HTML document is recommended for best client compatibility.
settings_item_mail_template_password_res: Subject template for password reset emails.
HTML template for password reset emails. A complete HTML document is recommended for best client compatibility.
Subject template for password reset confirmation emails.
HTML template for password reset confirmation emails. A complete HTML document is recommended for best client compatibility.
settings_item_mail_template_contact_chan: Subject template for previous-address email change notices.
HTML template for previous-address email change notices. A complete HTML document is recommended for best client compatibility.
settings_item_webdav_enabled_desc: Controls whether the WebDAV entrypoint is exposed. When disabled, clients can no longer access files through WebDAV.
settings_item_cors_enabled_desc: Controls whether AsterDrive actively processes cross-origin browser requests. When disabled, the server skips all CORS headers and enforcement.
settings_item_cors_allowed_origins_desc: List the browser origins that may access AsterDrive cross-origin, separated by commas. Empty means the server returns no CORS headers and the browser blocks cross-origin access naturally. "*" allows any origin.
settings_item_cors_allow_credentials_des: Controls whether CORS responses include Access-Control-Allow-Credentials. Do not combine this with an allowed-origins value of "*".
settings_item_cors_max_age_secs_desc: Controls how long browsers keep CORS preflight results cached before they need to re-check.
settings_item_mail_outbox_dispatch_inter: How often the server checks pending transactional email and tries to dispatch messages that are ready to send. Lower values send queued mail sooner but increase polling frequency.
settings_item_background_task_dispatch_i: How often the server checks the pending background-task queue and hands runnable work to workers, such as thumbnail generation and other asynchronous jobs. Lower values start queued work sooner but increase polling frequency.
Background task idle max scan interval
The longest interval the dispatcher may back off to after repeated idle scans. New tasks and manual retries still wake the dispatcher immediately.
settings_item_background_task_max_concur: Reserved fallback concurrency cap; currently unused until future task kinds are assigned to the fallback lane.
settings_item_background_task_archive_ma: The maximum number of archive compression and extraction tasks the server may execute at the same time. Higher values improve archive throughput but increase CPU, memory, and I/O pressure.
settings_item_background_task_thumbnail_: The maximum number of thumbnail generation tasks the server may execute at the same time. This lane is separate from archive work so archive backlogs do not block thumbnails.
settings_item_background_task_max_attemp: How many times regular workspace background tasks may be attempted before they become permanently failed. New tasks use this value, and manual retries reset failed tasks to the current setting; system periodic runtime events always stay fixed at 1.
settings_item_share_download_rollback_qu: Maximum buffered shared download rollback jobs before overflow aggregation is used.
settings_item_maintenance_cleanup_interv: How often the server runs periodic cleanup work for uploads, trash, locks, archived teams, audit logs, and task artifacts. Lower values clean up expired data sooner but make maintenance scans run more often.
settings_item_blob_reconcile_interval_se: How often the server runs a full consistency check between database references and underlying blob objects to detect missing references or orphaned stored data. Lower values find inconsistencies sooner but make the full scan run more often.
settings_item_remote_node_health_test_in: How often the server checks database, cache, and enabled remote nodes, then records the result as a system runtime event. Lower values surface failures sooner, but checks run more often.
settings_item_team_member_list_max_limit: The largest page size the admin and API endpoints allow when listing team members. Higher values show more results per request, but each query becomes heavier.
settings_item_task_list_max_limit_desc: The largest page size the admin and API endpoints allow when listing background tasks. Higher values show more results per request, but each query becomes heavier.
settings_item_max_versions_per_file_desc: The maximum number of historical versions kept for each file. Older versions are cleaned up once the limit is exceeded.
settings_item_trash_retention_days_desc: How long deleted files and folders stay in trash before they are permanently removed.
settings_item_team_archive_retention_day: How long archived teams are kept before they are permanently deleted.
settings_item_task_retention_hours_desc: How long temporary background task artifacts remain available before expiry; task records themselves are retained as history.
settings_item_default_storage_quota_desc: The default storage quota assigned to newly created users. Use 0 for unlimited.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_source: The largest uploaded ZIP source file allowed to enter online extraction. Lower values reduce central-directory memory pressure and decompression workload.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_stagin: The largest temporary disk footprint allowed while extracting a ZIP archive, counted as downloaded source archive bytes plus extracted staging bytes before import.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_uncomp: The largest total uncompressed file bytes accepted inside one ZIP archive before import.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_entrie: The maximum number of ZIP entries accepted during online extraction. This limits metadata-heavy archives with many tiny files or directories.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_files_: The maximum number of regular file entries accepted inside one ZIP archive.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_direct: The maximum number of directory paths accepted or implicitly created while extracting one ZIP archive.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_depth_: The deepest normalized ZIP entry path accepted during online extraction.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_path_b: The maximum UTF-8 byte length accepted for a normalized ZIP entry path.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_compre: The maximum total uncompressed-to-compressed byte ratio accepted for a ZIP archive.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_entry_: The maximum per-file uncompressed-to-compressed byte ratio accepted for ZIP entries.
settings_item_archive_extract_max_durati: The longest wall-clock runtime allowed for one online extraction task before the server stops it.
settings_item_archive_build_max_entries_: The maximum number of expanded file and directory entries allowed when creating ZIP archives for batch compression or download.
settings_item_archive_build_max_total_so: The maximum total source file bytes allowed when creating ZIP archives for batch compression or download.
settings_item_archive_build_max_temp_byt: The maximum estimated or actual ZIP output bytes allowed while creating archives, limiting temporary disk and streaming resource use.
settings_item_thumbnail_max_source_bytes: The largest source file size still allowed to enter thumbnail generation. Larger files are skipped so oversized originals do not overload thumbnail jobs.
settings_item_media_processing_registry_: Enable the media processors you want to use, bind extensions when needed, and let the AsterDrive built-in pipeline act as the final fallback when it is enabled.
settings_item_avatar_dir_desc: Local filesystem directory used for uploaded avatar files. Absolute paths are used as-is. Relative paths are resolved under the server's ./data directory.
settings_item_avatar_max_upload_size_byt: The largest avatar file users are allowed to upload. Oversized uploads are rejected, and requests are still bounded by the server's hard payload limit.
settings_item_audit_log_enabled_desc: Controls whether administrative and security-relevant actions are recorded. When disabled, no new audit records will be written.
settings_item_audit_log_retention_days_d: How long audit log entries are kept before they are permanently deleted.
settings_item_gravatar_base_url_desc: The base URL used to build Gravatar avatar requests. Replace it with a reachable proxy or mirror if the default service is inaccessible.
settings_item_public_site_url_desc: Set the public HTTP(S) origins that external users actually use to reach this site, one origin per field, for example https://drive.example.com and https://panel.example.com. They are used to build absolute links for share pages, Office previews, WebDAV, and callbacks; the first origin is the fallback. Leave the list empty to fall back to the current request origin.
settings_subcategory_general_preview_des: Manage the public preview app registry exposed to the frontend. This section edits the same JSON config using a structured list UI.
settings_subcategory_storage_archive_ext: Limit online ZIP extraction by source size, expanded size, entry count, path shape, and compression ratio to reduce zip-bomb and metadata-exhaustion risk.
settings_subcategory_storage_archive_bui: Limit expanded entry count, total source bytes, and ZIP output size for batch compression and archive downloads.
settings_subcategory_storage_media_proce: Configure available media processors, thumbnail limits, and optional vips or ffmpeg CLI integrations.
media_processing_validation_error: Fix the media processing rules before saving.
media_processing_editor_desc: Enable the processors you want, bind extensions when needed, and let the AsterDrive built-in pipeline handle the final fallback when it is enabled.
media_processing_editor_validation_title: Media processing config has validation issues
media_processing_editor_processor_enable: Controls whether this processor participates in thumbnail generation. Disabling it keeps the current settings for later reuse.
media_processing_editor_processor_extens: Only matching files will be routed into this processor.
media_processing_editor_processor_builti: AsterDrive built-in renderer. It does not need extension bindings, and it acts as the last fallback only when enabled.
The AsterDrive built-in processor does not need extension bindings. You can disable it completely; once disabled, it no longer participates in thumbnail processing.
media_processing_editor_processor_comman: Executable name or absolute path used when running this CLI processor. Only enable it when this command is available on the server.
media_processing_editor_rule_extensions_: Comma-separated suffixes like `heic, heif`. Leave empty to match any supported file.
media_processing_error_parse: Media processing config must be valid JSON.
media_processing_error_version_mismatch: Media processing config version must be {{version}}.
media_processing_error_no_enabled_proces: Enable at least one media processor.
settings_item_wopi_access_token_ttl_secs: How long a newly issued WOPI access token stays valid. Shorter values reduce replay exposure, but tokens that are too short can break long editor sessions.
settings_item_wopi_lock_ttl_secs_desc: How long an active WOPI lock remains valid before it expires automatically unless the client refreshes it.
settings_item_wopi_discovery_cache_ttl_s: How long fetched WOPI discovery metadata is reused before the server refreshes it from the configured discovery endpoint.
settings_item_frontend_preview_apps_json: Admin-managed JSON registry for the public file preview apps exposed to the frontend. Use it to enable, disable, reorder, or add preview providers such as custom URL templates and WOPI discovery imports.
preview_apps_editor_hint: This editor still writes back to the same JSON config, but it lets you edit apps, extension bindings, and provider settings directly instead of hand-writing JSON.
preview_apps_validation_error: Fix the preview app registry errors before saving.
cors_wildcard_credentials_validation_err: You cannot keep allowed origins as "*" while cross-origin credentials are enabled. Switch to an explicit origin list or turn credentials off before saving.
preview_apps_apps_section_desc: Each app is a preview provider. Configure its extension bindings and launch settings directly on the app itself.
preview_apps_add_dialog_desc: Choose how to add the app. Embed creates a manually configurable external preview app, while WOPI Discovery asks the server to fetch discovery metadata and generate the apps for you.
preview_apps_add_dialog_embed_desc: Create an external preview app that you configure manually with a URL template, extension bindings, and allowed origins.
preview_apps_add_dialog_wopi_desc: Provide a discovery URL and let the server parse the supported apps, icons, actions, and extensions into multiple WOPI app entries.
preview_apps_rules_section_desc: Rules decide which apps appear for matching files, and which one becomes the default open mode.
preview_apps_builtin_key_locked: This built-in app key is fixed and cannot be changed.
preview_apps_builtin_delete_disabled: This built-in app cannot be deleted
preview_apps_icon_hint: Leave this empty to use the default icon. To override it, provide a public image URL.
preview_apps_url_template_variables_hint: URL template values are URL-encoded automatically before insertion.
preview_apps_dialog_desc: Edit the app itself here, including extension bindings, icon overrides, localized names, and provider-specific settings.
preview_apps_url_template_variables_dial: These variables can be used in the URL template. The system URL-encodes values automatically, and {{file_preview_url}} requests a dedicated preview link only when needed.
preview_apps_url_template_variable_file_: The numeric ID of the current file.
The original name of the current file.
preview_apps_url_template_variable_mime_: The MIME type detected for the current file.
preview_apps_url_template_variable_size_: The current file size in bytes.
preview_apps_url_template_variable_downl: The same-origin relative download path for the current file.
The absolute download URL for the current file.
preview_apps_url_template_variable_file_: An absolute preview link suitable for external embeds such as Office or Google viewers. It is created only when this variable is used.
preview_apps_rule_matches_help: Leave a field empty to skip that dimension. Use commas to enter multiple values.
preview_apps_rule_apps_help: Choose which open methods should be shown when this rule matches.
preview_apps_matches_categories_desc: This uses the preview system's detected file categories, such as image, video, audio, pdf, document, and spreadsheet.
preview_apps_list_input_hint: Use commas to enter multiple values.
preview_apps_error_parse: The current draft could not be parsed as preview app config JSON.
preview_apps_error_version_mismatch: The preview app config version must stay at {{version}}.
preview_apps_error_no_apps: At least one preview app is required.
preview_apps_error_app_key_required: App {{index}} must define a non-empty key.
preview_apps_error_builtin_required: Built-in preview app "{{key}}" cannot be removed.
preview_apps_error_url_template_mode_req: URL template app {{index}} must define a mode.
preview_apps_error_url_template_required: URL template app {{index}} must define a URL template.
preview_apps_error_wopi_mode_required: WOPI app {{index}} must define an open mode.
preview_apps_error_wopi_target_required: WOPI app {{index}} must define an action URL or a discovery URL.
preview_apps_error_rule_apps_required: Rule {{index}} must reference at least one app.
preview_apps_error_rule_default_missing: Rule {{index}} default app "{{appKey}}" must also be selected in that rule.
preview_apps_wopi_mode_desc: Controls whether the WOPI host page stays embedded in the dialog or opens in a new tab.
preview_apps_wopi_discovery_dialog_desc: Send the discovery URL to the server so it can fetch the XML, parse the supported apps and extensions, and return an updated preview app draft.
preview_apps_wopi_discovery_success: Preview apps generated from WOPI Discovery
preview_apps_wopi_action_url_desc: Optional fixed host page URL. If left empty, the backend falls back to discovery.
preview_apps_wopi_discovery_url_desc: Optional discovery endpoint used to resolve the host page dynamically.
preview_apps_wopi_hint_desc: This config only declares how the WOPI host should be resolved. The backend still creates the launch session, signs the access token, and controls lock behavior when the file is opened.
preview_apps_wopi_hint_body: Use either a fixed action URL or a discovery URL here. Clients such as OnlyOffice still depend on the backend for the final launch URL, token lifetime, and WOPI lock semantics.
site_url_mismatch_title: Current site URL does not match the system config
site_url_mismatch_description: The current browser address is {{current}}, but the configured public site URL list does not include it (current config: {{configured}}). Update it now so share links, Office previews, WebDAV endpoints, and future callbacks use the current address?
settings_item_branding_title_desc: Controls the browser tab title shown on login, share, and app pages. Leave it empty to fall back to AsterDrive.
settings_item_branding_description_desc: Controls the HTML description metadata exposed before login. Leave it empty to fall back to the default deployment description.
settings_item_branding_favicon_url_desc: Provide a public HTTP(S) or same-origin icon URL. Leave it empty to fall back to /favicon.svg.
settings_item_branding_wordmark_dark_url: Site logo URL for light surfaces
Provide a public HTTP(S) or same-origin logo URL used on headers and other light surfaces. Leave it empty to fall back to /static/asterdrive/asterdrive-dark.svg.
settings_item_branding_wordmark_light_ur: Site logo URL for dark surfaces
Provide a public HTTP(S) or same-origin logo URL used on dark surfaces such as the login hero panel. Leave it empty to fall back to /static/asterdrive/asterdrive-light.svg.
settings_save_hint: Changes are staged as drafts until you save. Review them, then save together with ⌘/Ctrl + S.
audit_intro: Review important activity records and filter audit events by criteria.
about_intro: View the deployed version, product context, and related resources.
confirm_delete_policy: Are you sure you want to delete this policy?
confirm_force_delete: This will permanently delete the user and all their data.
no_filtered_audit_logs_desc: Try another action keyword or entity type.
confirm_purge: This will permanently delete the item. This cannot be undone.
confirm_empty_trash: This will permanently delete all items in trash.
no_shares_desc: Active share links will appear here.
no_teams_desc: Create a team or adjust the search keyword to see results here.
no_filtered_teams_desc: Try another keyword or switch back to active teams.
no_policies_desc: Storage policies control where files are stored.
no_policy_groups_desc: Policy groups let you route uploads across one or more storage policies.
custom_config_intro: Use this area for advanced overrides that are not part of the built-in system schema.
custom_config_empty: No custom config yet. Add entries here only when an additional override is necessary.
custom_config_key_required: New custom config entries must include a key.
custom_config_key_duplicate: That config key already exists. Use a different name.
custom_config_validation_error: Fix empty or duplicate custom config keys before saving.
custom_config_delete_staged: These custom config entries are marked for deletion and will only be removed after you save.
config_namespace_hint: Use namespace.name format, for example integrations.slack_theme
no_policies_assigned: No policy group assigned. Uploads will fail until one is set.
force_unlock_desc: This will release the WebDAV lock. Clients holding this lock will encounter errors.
delete_share_desc: After removal, the share link will no longer be available.
delete_policy_desc: This will not delete stored files, but new uploads using this policy will fail.
force_delete_policy_desc: This policy is still referenced by unfinished uploads. Continuing will clean up those upload sessions and temporary objects; deletion will still be rejected if stored file blobs use this policy.
delete_config_desc: This config entry will be permanently removed.
s3_endpoint_hint: For Cloudflare R2, use the account-level S3 endpoint such as https://<account>.r2.cloudflarestorage.com. Do not use a public .r2.dev URL; enter the bucket separately. If /<bucket> is included by mistake, the app will split and validate it automatically.
s3_endpoint_public_r2_dev_error: Detected a public r2.dev URL. This field must use the account-level Cloudflare R2 S3 API endpoint, not a public bucket domain.
s3_download_strategy_relay_stream_desc: AsterDrive fetches the object from S3 and streams it back to the browser. Use this when you need the app node to fully control response headers, same-origin delivery, or downstream network policy.
s3_download_strategy_presigned_desc: After AsterDrive completes permission checks, it redirects the browser to a short-lived S3 GET URL. This reduces app-node download bandwidth, but object storage now serves the final response and cache behavior.
remote_download_strategy_relay_stream_de: The primary node fetches the object from the follower and streams it back to the browser. Use this when you need the primary to keep full control of response headers, same-origin delivery, or downstream network policy.
remote_download_strategy_presigned_desc: After AsterDrive completes permission checks, it redirects the browser to a short-lived follower GET URL. This reduces primary-node download bandwidth, but the follower now serves the final response headers and cache behavior.
s3_upload_strategy_relay_stream_desc: The browser uploads to AsterDrive, and the server relays the bytes directly to S3. The normal path does not write local temporary files; only a small fallback path uses temp files. relay_stream does not perform SHA256 deduplication.
s3_upload_strategy_presigned_desc: Upload directly to S3 via presigned URLs. Files ≤ chunk size use single PUT; larger files use S3 multipart upload with resume support. This path does not perform SHA256 deduplication. Requires bucket CORS with ExposeHeaders: ["ETag"].
remote_upload_strategy_relay_stream_desc: The browser sends the full file to the primary, which streams it to the follower, and the follower writes it into the default landing target defined by the primary. No full-file temporary copy is created, but both network legs must stay healthy at the same time.
remote_upload_strategy_presigned_desc: The browser uploads directly to the follower via presigned URLs. Files ≤ chunk size use a single PUT; larger files use multipart direct upload with resume support and are composed inside the follower. No full-file temporary copy is created, but the follower must be reachable from the browser and expose the `ETag` response header.
local_content_dedup_desc: When enabled, local uploads compute SHA-256 after the temporary file is written and reuse blobs with identical content. When disabled, uploads skip the extra full-file read and always create a new blob.
chunk_size_desc: Files larger than this are split into chunks. For S3 relay_stream and presigned modes, and for remote presigned mode, this also controls the multipart part size; S3 multipart still obeys the 5 MiB minimum part size. 0 = always use single upload.
policy_test_failed_confirm_desc: The current S3 configuration failed the connection test. You can still save it, but uploads using this policy may fail. Do you want to continue?
policy_force_deleted: Policy deleted and unfinished uploads cleaned up
create_team_desc: Create a team as a system admin, choose the initial team admin, and bind exactly one storage policy group.
team_details_desc: Review the team overview, storage usage, and update the name, description, and policy group binding.
create_user_desc: Create a new account with the default quota and new-user default policy group.
team_search_placeholder: Search by team name or description...
team_admin_identifier_desc: Use an existing active user's username or email address. That user becomes the initial team admin.
team_policy_group_desc: Every team must keep exactly one active policy group. Only system admins can change it.
archive_team_desc: The team will be archived and disappear from active workspaces.
team_member_search_placeholder: Search members by username, email, or ID...
team_member_filtered_empty_desc: Try another keyword or adjust the filters.
team_members_readonly_archived: Archived teams stay readable, but member changes are disabled.
team_audit_desc: Track profile changes, membership updates, and archive or restore activity for this team.
team_audit_empty_desc: Team management events will appear here in reverse chronological order.
team_danger_zone_desc: Keep high-risk operations here so the impact stays obvious before execution.
team_danger_zone_hint: Confirm another manager is still available and notify members before archiving the team.
team_restore_danger_desc: Restoring the team makes it visible in active workspaces again and re-enables member access.
team_archive_confirm_label: Type the team name to confirm archiving
user_search_placeholder: Search by username, email, or ID...
no_filtered_users_desc: Try another keyword or adjust the filters.
storage_quota_settings_desc: Adjust the user's role, status, total quota, and policy group binding here.
security_actions_desc: Keep high-risk controls here, including password resets and forced sign-out.
admin_reset_password_desc: Set a new local sign-in password for this user without requiring their current password.
reset_password_hint: The new password must be 8-128 characters long.
revoke_sessions_desc: Invalidate this user's current access and refresh tokens across devices.
revoke_sessions_hint: Use this to force a fresh sign-in after a security event, account status change, or administrator intervention.
password_confirm_mismatch: The confirmation password does not match.
storage_policy_assignments_desc: Choose the default policy group for this user's uploads. Only one group can be bound at a time.
policy_group_invalid_assignment: The current policy group assignment is unavailable. Choose another active group or this user's uploads will fail.
policy_group_no_assignable_groups: No assignable policy groups are available yet. Create an enabled group with at least one rule first.
user_details_footer_hint: Changes are saved to the system immediately. Proceed carefully.
team_details_footer_hint: Changes are applied to the system immediately, including archive and restore actions.
initial_admin_delete_blocked: The initial admin account cannot be deleted.
initial_admin_status_blocked: The initial admin account cannot be disabled.
initial_admin_email_verification_blocked: The initial admin account email verification state cannot be changed.
initial_admin_role_blocked: The initial admin account cannot be demoted.
initial_policy_delete_blocked: The built-in policy with ID 1 cannot be deleted.
default_policy_required: A default policy must always exist while assignments remain.
default_policy_remove_blocked: Set another policy as default before removing the current default policy.
policy_wizard_step_storage_desc: Pick the storage backend for this policy.
policy_wizard_step_connection_desc: Set the S3 endpoint, bucket, and credentials.
policy_wizard_step_remote_desc: Choose the remote node that will store objects for this policy.
policy_wizard_step_local_desc: Name the policy and choose the local storage path.
policy_wizard_step_rules_desc: Adjust upload behavior and verify the final policy settings.
policy_wizard_choose_driver_desc: Start with where this policy stores uploads. You can refine connection details and limits in the next steps.
policy_wizard_local_storage_desc: Store files on the AsterDrive server filesystem. Simple setup and suitable for single-node deployments.
policy_wizard_remote_storage_desc: Store files on another AsterDrive node over the internal remote storage protocol. Good for tiered or federated deployments.
policy_wizard_s3_storage_desc: Store files in an S3-compatible object bucket such as Amazon S3, Cloudflare R2, or MinIO.
policy_wizard_bucket_required: Bucket is required for S3 policies.
policy_wizard_remote_node_required: Choose a remote node before continuing.
policy_wizard_remote_nodes_empty: Create and configure at least one remote node before saving a remote storage policy.
policy_wizard_s3_helper: Connection tests and upload strategy are available after the basic connection is filled in.
policy_wizard_remote_helper: The selected remote node handles network transport. This policy controls the remote path prefix, upload mode, and size limits.
policy_wizard_local_helper: Use a relative or absolute path. Leave it empty to fall back to the application's default data directory.
policy_wizard_remote_rules_helper: Remote storage does not use per-policy endpoint or credentials. Bind a remote node, choose pure stream relay or presigned direct upload, then configure upload limits and default-policy behavior.
policy_wizard_summary_desc: Review the key settings before saving. You can still go back and adjust any step.
policy_editor_overview_desc: Update the policy name, storage path, and review the active storage driver.
policy_editor_connection_desc: Adjust the endpoint, bucket, and credentials. Leaving secret fields empty keeps the current values.
policy_editor_remote_desc: Bind this policy to a configured remote node. Upload behavior and size limits are configured separately below.
policy_editor_rules_desc: Configure upload behavior, file size limits, and whether this should be the default policy.
policy_editor_summary_desc: Review the current configuration before saving.
delete_remote_node_desc: This removes the peer configuration. Any remote storage policies still bound to it must be reassigned first.
no_remote_nodes_desc: Register a follower node first. After saving it, you can immediately generate an enrollment command and run it on the follower server.
remote_node_enrollment_prepared: Remote node saved and enrollment command generated
remote_node_enrollment_completed_action_: This node has already completed enrollment and cannot generate another enrollment command
remote_node_overview_desc: Set the display name and outbound base URL for this peer.
remote_node_credentials_desc: These credentials are managed automatically for outbound signed requests to the peer. Manual edits are not supported in the current version; rotate will be added as a separate action later.
remote_node_routing_desc: Optional local policy used when the peer writes objects back into this node.
remote_node_enabled_desc: When disabled, the primary stops sending remote traffic to this node and the peer can no longer write objects into it.
remote_node_base_url_hint: Optional. If you already know the follower's public URL, add it here so primary-side remote policies can target it later. If not, leave it empty and finish enrollment first.
remote_node_base_url_invalid: Enter a valid base URL that starts with http:// or https://.
remote_node_status_settings_desc: This switch only decides whether the primary should keep using this node. When disabled, the primary stops sending remote traffic to it and the peer can no longer push objects into this node. Which local policy receives those objects is still written on the follower during enroll, not here.
remote_node_diagnostics_desc: Saved capability probe details from the latest connection test.
remote_node_test_failed_confirm_desc: The current remote node settings failed the connection test. You can still save them, but remote storage traffic may fail until the peer configuration is fixed.
remote_node_wizard_step_identity_desc: Set the display name. Policies and signed requests will reference this record later.
remote_node_wizard_step_connection_desc: Optionally fill in the follower public URL. Managed credentials are generated when the record is saved, and this step only prepares the primary-side enrollment record.
remote_node_wizard_step_routing_desc: Choose which local policy should receive objects when the peer writes them back into this node.
remote_node_wizard_step_review_desc: Review the primary-to-follower enrollment flow. The button below saves the primary-side record first, then generates the follower-side enroll command.
remote_node_wizard_name_required: Enter a name for this remote node.
remote_node_name_hint: Use an environment- or region-based name so the node is easier to identify later.
remote_node_wizard_access_key_required: Access Key is required before creation.
remote_node_wizard_secret_key_required: Secret Key is required before creation.
remote_node_wizard_routing_helper_title: What does receive policy mean here?
remote_node_wizard_routing_helper_desc: When the peer writes objects back into this node, the selected local policy handles them first. Leaving it empty keeps the node record without assigning a default landing target.
remote_node_wizard_auto_credentials_desc: Access Key and Secret Key are managed by the system and generated on save, so they do not need to be entered manually. After save, they are bundled into the one-time enrollment command for the follower.
remote_node_wizard_followup_value: Generate the enroll command after saving the record
remote_node_wizard_docs_desc: If this follower will run in Docker, follow the bootstrap-ENV guide directly instead of manually entering the container to run enroll.
remote_node_primary_site_url_required: Set the primary public site URL in system settings before creating a remote node. The enrollment command needs a stable primary URL.
remote_node_enrollment_dialog_desc: The primary-side follower record has already been saved. Run the command below on the follower server to write the local master binding. Landing targets are configured separately from the primary UI.
remote_node_enrollment_flow_desc: You do not need both admin consoles open at the same time. Generate the command here, then run one enroll command on the follower server.
remote_node_enrollment_step_issue_desc: This command already includes the primary URL and enrollment token. Until the final ack succeeds and the session expires, the same command can be retried if something fails.
remote_node_enrollment_step_run_title: Run enroll on the follower server
remote_node_enrollment_step_run_desc: The CLI redeems bootstrap settings from the primary node, writes the local master binding on the follower, and flips runtime mode to follower.
remote_node_enrollment_step_restart_desc: This version writes configuration into the database, but the running service does not hot-reload remote nodes yet, so restart is still required.
remote_node_enrollment_command_desc: Copy this command to the follower server and run it there. If you later wrap this in a shell script, this can be the underlying command.
remote_node_enrollment_command_hint: Append `--database-url` only if the follower CLI must target a specific local database. Landing targets are now configured separately from this page on the primary side.
remote_node_enrollment_verify_desc: If you have already run enroll on the follower server and restarted the service, you can verify primary-to-follower connectivity right here.
remote_node_enrollment_verify_hint: A follower `base_url` is already configured, so this verification can run immediately. If it fails, continue checking the URL, certificate, or firewall configuration.
remote_node_enrollment_verify_disabled_h: No follower `base_url` is configured yet, so the primary cannot actively verify connectivity right now. Add the address first if you want to run the test.
ingress_policy_hint: Applied to objects accepted from this peer. Remote storage policies cannot be selected as ingress targets.
remote_node_ingress_profiles_desc: Primary decides where follower-side incoming objects land here. Local paths stay under the follower managed ingress root, multi-primary followers reject this mode, and remote writes stay blocked until a default landing target exists.
remote_node_ingress_profiles_base_url_re: Save a reachable follower `base_url` first. Primary-side ingress profile management proxies through the follower API.
remote_node_ingress_profiles_empty_desc: Create at least one local or S3 target and set a default, or this follower will refuse remote writes.
remote_node_ingress_profile_form_desc: Only local and S3 drivers are supported. The default profile becomes the landing target for managed follower ingress.
remote_node_ingress_profile_base_path_re: Base path is required for local ingress profiles.
Local base path must stay relative and cannot escape the managed ingress root.
remote_node_ingress_profile_endpoint_req: Endpoint is required for S3 ingress profiles.
remote_node_ingress_profile_bucket_requi: Bucket is required for S3 ingress profiles.
remote_node_ingress_profile_access_key_r: Access Key is required for S3 ingress profiles.
remote_node_ingress_profile_secret_key_r: Secret Key is required for S3 ingress profiles.
remote_node_ingress_profile_max_file_siz: Max file size must be a non-negative integer.
remote_node_ingress_profile_local_path_h: Relative only. The follower resolves this path under its managed ingress root.
remote_node_ingress_profile_s3_path_hint: Optional object prefix inside the bucket.
remote_node_ingress_profile_local_scope_: This profile writes into follower-local storage. The base path above is always resolved relative to `server.follower.managed_ingress_local_root`, never as an arbitrary host path.
remote_node_ingress_profile_default_togg: Use as the default ingress profile
remote_node_ingress_profile_default_hint: Managed follower ingress always lands on the default profile.
remote_node_ingress_profile_default_lock: This profile is already default. To change it, set another profile as default first.
remote_node_ingress_profile_credentials_: These credentials are required and will be stored on the follower.
Leave blank to keep the existing S3 credentials.
remote_node_ingress_profile_delete_desc: This removes the managed ingress target from the follower. Make another profile default first if this one is still the default and others remain.
policy_group_rules_desc: Rules are checked by ascending priority. File size ranges are optional and use MB in the form.
policy_group_policy_search_placeholder: Filter by policy name, ID, or driver...
policy_group_no_filtered_policies: No policies match the current filter.
policy_group_rule_hint: Pick a policy, assign a unique priority, and optionally constrain the file size range.
policy_group_description_placeholder: Optional notes about how this group should be used
policy_group_overview_desc: Set the group name, keep a short description, and decide whether it should be enabled by default.
policy_group_enabled_desc: Disabled groups cannot be assigned to users. Move any assigned users away before disabling a group.
policy_group_default_desc: Assigned automatically when creating new users. Runtime uploads do not fall back if a user has no policy group.
policy_group_migration_desc: Move every user currently bound to the source group into another policy group.
policy_group_migration_hint: This only updates users' policy_group_id values.
policy_group_migration_target_required: Choose a target policy group first.
policy_group_migration_same_group_invali: The target group must be different from the source group.
policy_group_migration_unavailable: Create another policy group before using migration.
delete_policy_group_desc: This will remove the group and its routing rules. Assigned users must be moved first.
policy_group_dialog_desc: Define an ordered rule set that maps uploads to storage policies by file size.
policy_group_default_requires_enabled: A default policy group must remain enabled.
policy_group_rule_required: At least one routing rule is required.
policy_group_rule_policy_required: Each rule must select a storage policy.
policy_group_rule_priority_invalid: Each rule priority must be a positive integer.
policy_group_rule_priority_duplicate: Rule priorities must be unique within the group.
policy_group_rule_policy_duplicate: A storage policy can only appear once in the same group.
policy_group_rule_size_invalid: File size limits must be valid non-negative numbers.
policy_group_rule_range_invalid: The max file size must be greater than the min file size.
policy_group_delete_default_blocked: Set another group as default before deleting the current default group.
policy_group_no_policies_available: Create at least one storage policy before creating a policy group.
about_tagline: A self-hosted control plane for files, sharing, storage policies, and runtime configuration.
about_summary: AsterDrive ships the Rust backend and React admin panel through one delivery chain, balancing single-binary deployment, object storage extensibility, WebDAV access, and an operations-friendly management surface.
about_resources_desc: Documentation, repository, and license links are available here.
